rulings.Case Title: GLORIA TRAN VS. BAYVIEW CAPITAL, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Tax Costs
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, November 30, 2011, Line 1, PLAINTIFF GLORIA TRAN Notice Of Motion To Tax Costs is Granted. While neither side provided the court with a courtesy copy of the memorandum of costs, from what the court is able to deduce, there is no legal basis for the claimed expert costs and defendant provided an insufficient evidentiary basis to support its claimed deposition costs. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/HEK

rulings.Case Title: CONTRACTORS ACCESS PROGRAM OF CALIFORNIA, A VS. MAJESTIC CAPITAL, LTD., A FOREIGN CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, November 30, 2011, Line 8, DEFENDANT TWIN BRIDGES (BERMUDA), LTD., DANIEL HICKEY, CHESTER WALCZYK DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Overruled as to the first cause of action as to Hickey and Walczyk, sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the first, third, fourth and fifth causes of action against Twin Bridges and the fifth cause of action against Hickey and Walczyk. Plaintiff is granted leave to amend, if it can do so in good faith, that: Twin Bridges owed it a fiduciary duty, 2) Twin Bridges knowingly was unjustly enriched, 3) Twin Bridges¿ conduct violated one of the prongs of section 17200, and 4) all three demurring defendants negligently made a misrepresentation that was relied on by plaintiff. The face of the first amended complaint does not plainly show that any of the claims alleged against the demurring party is time-barred. The complaint adequately pleads a breach of fiduciary claim against Hickey and Walczyk. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: CONTRACTORS ACCESS PROGRAM OF CALIFORNIA, A VS. MAJESTIC CAPITAL, LTD., A FOREIGN CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion Andm Otion To Transfer And Consolidate
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, November 30, 2011, Line 8, PLAINTIFF CONTRACTORS ACCESS PROGRAM OF CALIFORNIA's Notice Of Motion And Motion To Transfer And Consolidate. The motion was continued from November 17 to be heard with the discovery motion on calendar today. The court re-affirms its previous tentative ruling. Denied. Common questions of fact and law do not predominate. Nor does the proposed transfer and consolidation further judicial economy or the convenience of the parties, witnesses and counsel. Any concern regarding conflicting privilege rulings can be handled by other means. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Motion To Have Third Party Witness Theodora Orhinger Pc Held In Contempt Or/For An Order To Appear For Depositin; Request For Sanctions Against Theodora Oringher Pc Or Theodora Oringher Pc
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, November 30, 2011, Line 9, DEFENDANTs MARTIN BROS./MARCOWALL, INC., A CALIFORNIA and MOHAMMAD CHAHINE's Motion To Have Third Party Witness Theodora Orhinger Pc Held In Contempt Or/For An Order To Appear For Depositin; Request For Sanctions Against Theodora Oringher Pc Or Theodora Oringher Pc is Granted as follows: No later than December 23, 2011, non-party Theodora Orhinger is required to produce all responsive documents in its custody, possession and control and to designate one or more representatives who can be deposed about the documents. The deposition(s) must occur at a mutually agreeable date and location no later than January 31, 2012. The responsive documents fall within the scope of discovery relevance and non-party Theodora Orhinger has failed to satisfy its burden that any of the documents are still privileged. rulings.Case Title: PATRICK F. BARRON VS. ADAMS AND ASSOCIATES, INC., WHICH WILL DO BUSINESS
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Costs And Attorneys Fees Pursuant To Labor Code
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, November 30, 2011, Line 15, DEFENDANT ADAMS AND ASSOCIATES, INC., WHICH WILL DO BUSINESS IN CALIFORNIA AS ADAMS AND ASSOCIATES OF NV, INC.'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Costs And Attorneys Fees Pursuant To Labor Code. Granted in part. Respondent is awarded fees and costs of $6450 (reasonable fees of $6000 plus costs of $450). Appellant should not be required to pay the fees incurred for respondent¿s motion to continue which was for respondent¿s convenience. Appellant¿s opposition is essentially a request to vacate the order granting the motion to strike the appeal. However, that request requires a motion for reconsideration or a motion to vacate, which has not been filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)
